entries.acceptedLesson: Already exposed as a normal skill for SCF positioning workflows.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not convert positioning-only guidance into any SCF submission/write capability.
entries.rationale: Public, read-only LumenLoop skill maps to exposed LumenLoop read operations and improves search/execute planning without transport onboarding.

entries.acceptedLesson: Already exposed for idea-to-primitives and prior-art checks.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not surface upstream connector setup or non-exposed LumenLoop skill cross-links.
entries.rationale: Public skill uses read-only discovery workflows and current exposed LumenLoop operations.

entries.acceptedLesson: Already exposed for claim checking, canonical names, handles, and citations.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not expose private source packs or partner-only research request flows.
entries.rationale: Public skill consumes public project/content data and is useful as a user-readable playbook.

entries.acceptedLesson: Already exposed and also has a Raven-owned runnable core for digest generation.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not mention retired onboarding skill ids or upstream connector setup from cross-links.
entries.rationale: Public read-only workflow aligns with Raven's exposed operations and current runner contract.

entries.acceptedLesson: Already exposed for sector/topic landscape mapping.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not treat the legacy duplicate copies as separate resources.
entries.rationale: Public read-only skill remains useful and has canonical current mirror coverage.

entries.acceptedLesson: Already exposed for choosing ecosystem dependencies to integrate.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not invent integrations outside returned public data.
entries.rationale: Public read-only skill maps to project/content lookup and integration selection.

entries.acceptedLesson: Whole skill stays exposed; the older runnable implementation was retired separately because it was unused catalog noise.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not re-add the retired dossier runner from prior code unless a new runnable use case is proven.
entries.rationale: The readable playbook is public and useful; execution is handled through generic exposed operations instead of a hidden raw-skill path.

entries.acceptedLesson: No new emitted text accepted in this pass. Keep only the existing Raven-owned understanding that LumenLoop calls go through wrapped host-side globals, not direct connector setup.
entries.rejectedLessons: Connector installation, MCP troubleshooting for Claude Desktop, direct MCP endpoint setup, upstream tool-map instructions, bearer/key/rate-limit/account advice.
entries.rationale: Raw body is public but misleading inside Raven's no-network sandbox. Existing v1 classification is sufficient and remains non-emitted.

entries.canonicalSource: LumenLoop partner API name-only skill stub; legacy bodies exist only as read-only prior art
entries.acceptedLesson: No new Raven-facing guidance accepted.
entries.rejectedLessons: Budget dashboards, metering, top-ups, HTTP 402 recovery, x402 top-up instructions, quota increase requests.
entries.rationale: Partner/account/billing guidance is not suitable for the public repo or model-facing emitted surfaces.

entries.canonicalSource: LumenLoop partner API name-only skill stub; legacy bodies exist only as read-only prior art
entries.acceptedLesson: No new Raven-facing guidance accepted.
entries.rejectedLessons: REST onboarding, Bearer auth, tier ladder, first smoke test, direct 401/403/429 setup guidance.
entries.rationale: Direct REST onboarding contradicts Raven's host-side secrets and sandbox globals.

entries.canonicalSource: LumenLoop partner API name-only skill stub; legacy bodies exist only as read-only prior art
entries.acceptedLesson: No new Raven-facing guidance accepted; existing Raven envelope/error guidance already covers the safe portion.
entries.rejectedLessons: OpenAPI typed-client generation, direct REST clients, raw REST envelope handling, partner rate-limit instructions.
entries.rationale: The safe lesson is already Raven-owned and the raw partner body would leak a non-Raven integration model.

entries.canonicalSource: LumenLoop partner API name-only skill stub; legacy bodies exist only as read-only prior art
entries.acceptedLesson: No new Raven-facing guidance accepted.
entries.rejectedLessons: Listing credentials, minting llmcp_ keys, labels, expiries, revocation, leaked-key workflows.
entries.rationale: Key management is private/account-mutating and must never enter model-facing Raven surfaces.

entries.canonicalSource: LumenLoop partner API name-only skill stub; legacy bodies exist only as read-only prior art
entries.acceptedLesson: No new Raven-facing guidance accepted; current operation descriptions already encode short keyword search, semantic search, slug follow-up, and envelope handling.
entries.rejectedLessons: Plain HTTP scripts, direct REST tool calling, guest-tier REST catalog wiring, upstream raw envelope names.
entries.rationale: The potentially useful operation-selection advice is already present in Raven-owned notes; raw body remains partner/private provenance.

entries.canonicalSource: LumenLoop partner API name-only skill stub; legacy bodies exist only as read-only prior art
entries.acceptedLesson: No new Raven-facing guidance accepted.
entries.rejectedLessons: Metered research commissioning, request_research polling, source packs, schema fills, paid-call recovery.
entries.rationale: Paid partner research remains excluded until a separate budget/approval ADR deliberately enables the whole lane.

entries.acceptedLesson: Canonical SDF smart-contract skill supersedes old Soroban copies.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not resurrect the retired terminal id as a separate exposed skill.
entries.rationale: Official current SDF skill set has seven skills and uses smart-contracts for Soroban contract development.

entries.acceptedLesson: No separate lesson; current smart-contracts content and Stellar Docs carry the maintained Soroban guidance.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not keep a parallel terminal skill id or copy stale CLI/protocol snippets from legacy repos.
entries.rationale: Legacy trees and older marketplace cards used soroban, but the official current body is smart-contracts and is already exposed.

entries.acceptedLesson: Already exposed as canonical SDF guidance.
entries.rejectedLessons: Exclude copied legacy/cf-mastra/vercel-eve duplicates.
entries.rationale: Official current SDF skill and useful user-facing build playbook.

entries.acceptedLesson: Already exposed for OpenZeppelin Stellar project setup.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not expose non-Stellar OpenZeppelin Cairo/Solidity/Stylus skills in this gateway.
entries.rationale: Official OpenZeppelin Stellar subset is exactly the three Stellar contract skills.

entries.acceptedLesson: Already exposed for secure Stellar contract patterns.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not duplicate legacy copies from older repos.
entries.rationale: Official OpenZeppelin Stellar subset is public and useful.

entries.acceptedLesson: Already exposed for upgradeable Stellar contract workflows.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not duplicate legacy copies from older repos.
entries.rationale: Official OpenZeppelin Stellar subset is public and useful.

entries.acceptedLesson: No Raven-specific lesson accepted.
entries.rejectedLessons: Non-Stellar setup/upgrade workflows are outside this Stellar gateway's skill surface.
entries.rationale: Current mirror deliberately includes only the Stellar subset.

entries.acceptedLesson: Already exposed. Upstream HEAD 282322e adds partner/provider lookup guidance: use returned partner directory results only, never invent providers; propose a separate mirror refresh and focused routing case.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not use stale legacy Scout variants as separate resources; do not expose write/feedback endpoints excluded by ADR-0003.
entries.rationale: Canonical Scout skill is public and exposed. Current upstream drift is Raven-safe in principle but was not emitted in this pass.

entries.acceptedLesson: Candidate for orchestrator review: developer-activity answers should pair any numeric activity/leaderboard evidence with a second project/content corroboration hop, and avoid citing provider-hosted internal URLs.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not expose the legacy ecosystem-records adapter endpoint or its provider URL; do not add a hidden leaderboard op.
entries.rationale: Unique legacy body contains a safe evidence-quality lesson, but its endpoint is not part of the current Raven manifest. Existing Scout body text still names this external companion; changing that emitted text is outside this shard and should be reviewed by the orchestrator.

entries.acceptedLesson: No additional lesson beyond the canonical exposed LumenLoop public skills.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not review or emit every legacy generated/src copy as a separate skill.
entries.rationale: Canonical current mirror wins; legacy copies under sibling repos are provenance only.

entries.canonicalSource: current public mirror ecosystem-skills/skills/stellar-light/stellar-scout and current upstream Stellar-Light/stellar-scout
entries.acceptedLesson: No additional lesson beyond canonical Scout plus the upstream drift note on the exposed Scout row.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not expose stale legacy Scout copies or generated skill copies.
entries.rationale: Legacy copies are superseded by the current Scout source.

entries.canonicalSource: current public mirror ecosystem-skills/skills/stellar-dev and official stellar/stellar-dev-skill
entries.acceptedLesson: No additional lesson beyond the canonical SDF exposed skills.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not review generated/cache copies as separate current sources.
entries.rationale: Duplicate terminal skill bodies are copied from older SDF/Soroban sources; canonical current source wins.

entries.canonicalSource: current public mirror ecosystem-skills/skills/openzeppelin-stellar and official OpenZeppelin/openzeppelin-skills
entries.acceptedLesson: No additional lesson beyond the canonical exposed OpenZeppelin Stellar skills.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not expose duplicate legacy copies.
entries.rationale: Canonical OpenZeppelin source wins and the local mirror already exposes the Stellar subset.

entries.acceptedLesson: Use as repo-agent guidance only when working on Cloudflare Agents internals; no MCP-delivered user resource.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not merge Cloudflare SDK docs into Stellar Raven's searchable skill surface.
entries.rationale: Framework docs are operational implementation guidance for agents working on the repo, not a Stellar ecosystem capability exposed by Raven.

entries.acceptedLesson: The durable process lesson is already in this inventory: canonical source wins, generated/cache duplicates are excluded, partner bodies stay private.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not expose the repo's mirror-maintenance instructions as a user skill.
entries.rationale: This is build-agent guidance for maintaining the mirror, not a runtime/public Raven capability.

entries.acceptedLesson: Repo-only lesson: require clustered evidence before changing guidance. This pass records candidates for orchestrator review instead of editing emitted text.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not expose eval-fixing workflow as a user resource.
entries.rationale: The skill is about maintaining Raven, not answering Stellar ecosystem users.

entries.acceptedLesson: Repo-only lesson: eval conclusions need dated artifacts and reviewer evidence.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not expose internal eval orchestration instructions to Raven users.
entries.rationale: Evaluation governance belongs in repo process, not model-facing catalog/bundle text.

entries.acceptedLesson: Repo-only lesson: sync scripts should fail closed on unclassified skills and preserve publish-safety rules.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not expose update scripts, credential sourcing, or deploy workflow as an MCP skill.
entries.rationale: Operational maintenance playbook for source syncs; not a user-facing Stellar capability.

entries.acceptedLesson: No Raven-specific catalog/search lesson accepted.
entries.rejectedLessons: Flue docs crawling and framework sync are unrelated to this Stellar gateway surface.
entries.rationale: Framework-specific operational docs sync for a retired sibling repo.

entries.acceptedLesson: No Raven-specific catalog/search lesson accepted.
entries.rejectedLessons: Mastra framework API verification belongs to the retired cf-mastra project, not Raven's emitted skill surface.
entries.rationale: Framework development guide unrelated to current Cloudflare codemode gateway.

entries.acceptedLesson: No Raven-specific catalog/search lesson accepted.
entries.rejectedLessons: Flue framework documentation routing is unrelated to Raven's emitted Stellar skill surface.
entries.rationale: Framework-specific skill from a retired sibling repo.

entries.acceptedLesson: No lesson accepted.
entries.rejectedLessons: Example greeting/review skills, generated skill outputs, Eve workspace resources, sandbox caches, dev-runtime snapshots.
entries.rationale: These are examples, generated outputs, or cache snapshots. Canonical source rows above cover any real skill bodies they duplicate.

entries.acceptedLesson: No lesson accepted.
entries.rejectedLessons: Dependency package skills are unrelated to Raven's curated Stellar ecosystem source map.
entries.rationale: node_modules is not a canonical source for this inventory and contains dependency docs/examples only.

entries.acceptedLesson: No raw skill-body lesson; these remain directory/catalog context rather than mirrored SKILL.md bodies.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not invent skill.read bodies for SDK, CLI, tool, or MCP-server cards.
entries.rationale: Directory cards are not mirrored skill bodies and do not belong in the skill exposure classification ledger.

entries.canonicalSource: skills.stellar.org community card listing noted in Solo comment
entries.acceptedLesson: No raw skill-body lesson accepted in this pass.
entries.rejectedLessons: Do not use the SDF marketplace as the sole source of truth for community skill bodies.
entries.rationale: Comment 2254 explicitly says the marketplace is not the sole source for community ecosystem/research skills; use maintainer repos/APIs instead.
